source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Inelastic neutrino scattering in the quark model. Consider the scattering of neutrinos on free, massless quarks. We will simplify things and discuss only strangeness-conserving reactions, i.e. transitions only between the $u$ and $d$ quarks. The experimental value is $\sigma^{\bar{u} N} / \sigma^{u N} = 0.37 \pm 0.02$. What does this value tell you about the quark/antiquark structure of the nucleon?
extraction_notes: answer field is numeric only; reasoning indicates it is the antiquark-to-quark ratio $\alpha$
image entries are serialized PIL object strings rather than actual file paths or URLs

issues: metadata.image_paths contains serialized PIL object strings rather than actual file paths or URLs

image_dependency: Asset registry shows one image exists in `image_manifest` with readable dimensions and nontrivial file size.
Question text itself does not explicitly reference a figure or visual element, so image dependence is supported by metadata more than by the wording.
multi_step: Question asks the solver to interpret an experimental ratio `sigma^{bar{u} N} / sigma^{u N} = 0.37 +- 0.02` and infer nucleon quark/antiquark structure.
This requires combining the stated scattering setup, the measured ratio, and a conceptual conclusion about parton content rather than extracting a single fact.
The prompt is open-ended and physics-reasoning heavy, indicating more than one reasoning step.
verifiability: There is a format mismatch: the prompt asks `What does this value tell you ...` which suggests an explanatory answer, but `answer_text` is the numeric string `$4 \\times 10^{-2}$`.
Top-level `answer_type` is `short_text`, while `asset_registry_record.answer_manifest.answer_type` is `number`, which reduces confidence in answer normalization.
Although `registry_passed=true` and an answer is present, the apparent question-answer mismatch makes automatic checking less reliable.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Inelastic neutrino scattering in the quark model. Consider the scattering of neutrinos on free, massless quarks. We will simplify things and discuss only strangeness-conserving reactions, i.e. transitions only between the $u$ and $d$ quarks. The experimental value is $\sigma^{\bar{u} N} / \sigma^{u N} = 0.37 \pm 0.02$. What does this value tell you about the quark/antiquark structure of the nucleon?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: answer field is numeric only; reasoning indicates it is the antiquark-to-quark ratio $\alpha$
image entries are serialized PIL object strings rather than actual file paths or URLs
metadata.reasoning_chain:
The experimental value $\sigma\left(\bar{u} N\right) / \sigma\left(u N\right)=0.37 \pm 0.02$ is approximately the same as the theoretical value $1 / 3$. This means that nucleons consist mainly of quarks, any antiquarks present would be very small in proportion. Let the ratio of antiquark to quark in a nucleon be $\alpha$, then
$\frac{\sigma\left(\bar{u} N\right)}{\sigma\left(u N\right)}=\frac{3 \sigma\left(\bar{u} u\right)+3 \alpha \sigma\left(\bar{u} \bar{d}\right)}{3 \sigma\left(u d\right)+3 \alpha \sigma\left(u \bar{u}\right)}=\frac{3 \times \frac{1}{3}+3 \alpha \times 1}{3 \times 1+3 \alpha \times \frac{1}{3}}=\frac{1+3 \alpha}{3+\alpha}=0.37$
giving
$\frac{1+3 \alpha}{8}=\frac{0.37}{2.63}$
or
$\alpha=4 \times 10^{-2}$

sentence_segments.text: Inelastic neutrino scattering in the quark model.
Consider the scattering of neutrinos on free, massless quarks.
We will simplify things and discuss only strangeness-conserving reactions, i.e.
transitions only between the $u$ and $d$ quarks.
The experimental value is $\sigma^{\bar{u} N} / \sigma^{u N} = 0.37 \pm 0.02$.
What does this value tell you about the quark/antiquark structure of the nucleon?

We will simplify things and discuss only strangeness-conserving reactions, i.e. transitions only between the $u$ and $d$ quarks. problem_main_record.normalized_question_text: Inelastic neutrino scattering in the quark model. Consider the scattering of neutrinos on free, massless quarks. We will simplify things and discuss only strangeness-conserving reactions, i.e. transitions only between the $u$ and $d$ quarks. The experimental value is $\sigma^{\bar{u} N} / \sigma^{u N} = 0.37 \pm 0.02$. What does this value tell you about the quark/antiquark structure of the nucleon?
problem_main_record.reasoning_chain:
The experimental value $\sigma\left(\bar{u} N\right) / \sigma\left(u N\right)=0.37 \pm 0.02$ is approximately the same as the theoretical value $1 / 3$. This means that nucleons consist mainly of quarks, any antiquarks present would be very small in proportion. Let the ratio of antiquark to quark in a nucleon be $\alpha$, then
$\frac{\sigma\left(\bar{u} N\right)}{\sigma\left(u N\right)}=\frac{3 \sigma\left(\bar{u} u\right)+3 \alpha \sigma\left(\bar{u} \bar{d}\right)}{3 \sigma\left(u d\right)+3 \alpha \sigma\left(u \bar{u}\right)}=\frac{3 \times \frac{1}{3}+3 \alpha \times 1}{3 \times 1+3 \alpha \times \frac{1}{3}}=\frac{1+3 \alpha}{3+\alpha}=0.37$
giving
$\frac{1+3 \alpha}{8}=\frac{0.37}{2.63}$
or
$\alpha=4 \times 10^{-2}$
